mission.title: Install and Configure Oxia on Kubernetes
mission.description: Oxia is a scalable metadata store and coordination system designed for large-scale distributed systems. It provides a ZooKeeper-compatible API with horizontal scalability. Installing Oxia via Helm deploys a StatefulSet-based cluster with configurable replication.

steps.title: Add the Oxia Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Oxia Helm chart repository:
```bash
helm repo add oxia https://oxia-db.github.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Create a namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for the Oxia cluster:
```bash
kubectl create namespace oxia
```

steps.title: Install Oxia using Helm
steps.description:
Install the Oxia cluster chart in the oxia namespace:
```bash
helm install oxia oxia/oxia-cluster --namespace oxia
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Oxia server and coordinator pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oxia
kubectl get svc -n oxia
```
The primary client endpoint is on port 6648.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show Oxia server (StatefulSet) and coordinator (Deployment) pods running in the oxia namespace. The client endpoint is available on port 6648.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add oxia https://oxia-db.github.io/helm-charts/
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove Oxia
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Helm release and clean up:
```bash
helm uninstall oxia --namespace oxia
kubectl delete namespace oxia
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Oxia
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repo and upgrade:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade oxia oxia/oxia-cluster --namespace oxia
kubectl get pods -n oxia
```

troubleshooting.title: Server pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Oxia servers run as a StatefulSet requiring PersistentVolumes. Check if a StorageClass is available:
```bash
kubectl get sc
kubectl describe pod -n oxia -l app=oxia-server
```

troubleshooting.title: Connection refused on port 6648
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the service is running and port-forward for testing:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n oxia
kubectl port-forward svc/oxia-server -n oxia 6648:6648
```

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster with a StorageClass for persistent volumes and Helm 3 installed.
